name: Cdc42 inhibition rejuvenated aged mouse HSC phenotypes
summary: Mouse HSC evidence supports Cdc42 inhibition as a mechanistic stem-cell rejuvenation axis.
statement: In aged mouse HSCs, pharmacologic Cdc42 inhibition with CASIN restored cell polarity and histone H4K16 acetylation distribution toward young-cell patterns and improved functional HSC readouts.
time_horizon: Ex vivo and transplant-oriented mouse assays
quantitative_note: The key public use is direction and mechanism, not a generalizable treatment effect size.
caveats: The strongest functional evidence remains preclinical.
HSC rejuvenation readouts do not directly establish broad organismal aging benefit.
Cdc42 inhibition safety and target engagement in humans remain unresolved.
